scenario: Ridge Healthcare runs 2500 records per day through a triage step in Camunda 8.8 where any subset of inner diagnostics tasks may be executed in any order under a runtime-decided FEEL completion condition. There is no fixed sequence flow that pre-determines which inner tasks run.
question: Which BPMN subprocess variant fits a flexible inner set of tasks completed under a FEEL completion condition with no fixed sequence?

options.text: Ad-hoc subprocess holds a set of inner activities with no required sequence and completes when the configured FEEL completion condition becomes true
Embedded subprocess holds a set of inner activities with no required sequence and completes when its inner end events have all been reached
Event subprocess holds a set of inner activities with no required sequence and completes when its inner start event fires for the parent
Call activity holds a set of inner activities with no required sequence and completes when the referenced child process finishes its main flow

a.text: Correct. Ad-hoc subprocesses execute a flexible set of inner activities in any order and complete on a configured completion condition, which fits the runtime-decided triage step.

b.text: Incorrect. Embedded subprocesses run inner activities along their sequence flows; they do not support unordered execution by definition.

c.text: Incorrect. Event subprocesses are triggered by an internal start event in a parent scope; they do not model an unordered task set.

d.text: Incorrect. Call activities invoke another deployed process definition; the unordered task set is not a property of call activities.

explanation: Ad-hoc subprocesses model an unordered set of inner activities completed under a configured completion condition, which is the documented flexible flow pattern.
